{"decision": "fail", "risk": "medium", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 75, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Duct joints are unsealed.", "Cloth duct tape is present instead of code-approved mastic or foil tape."], "actions": ["Remove cloth duct tape from all duct joints.", "Seal all duct joints with approved mastic or UL 181A-P/B-FX listed foil tape per SMACNA and project specifications.", "Re-inspect all supply, return, and exhaust duct joints before closure."], "rationale": "IMC 603 and SMACNA require duct joints and seams to be sealed to prevent air leakage. Cloth duct tape is not an approved sealant for ductwork. Unsealed joints violate code and reduce system efficiency and indoor air quality control.", "references": ["IMC 603", "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards", "ACCA Manual D"]}
